A man wearing glasses and a leather jacket sits on a park bench. Rose, Ethan's grown-up daughter, approaches him.
Rose looks at him in surprise.
They go into his lab.
He points to a voluminous container with a red mushroom inside.

She brings her hand to the container. The mushrooms inside it begin to glow. Rose plunges into the ocean, which is an allegory for the depths of her consciousness. She hears the voices of people she knows. She floats past the windows behind which the figures stand.
She covers her ears with her hands. The voices keep coming from all directions. Rose sinks deeper and deeper, to the point she can no longer stand the voices.
She comes to her senses sitting on a chair in the lab.
The lab seems a little different. The light is dimmer.
She has the feeling that something has happened here. She walks to the exit and sees a note on the board.
Rose opens the door from the lab and sees…that everything around her is infested with fungus.

She walks down a corridor whose walls are crooked with time. We realize that she is not walking through the corridors of the laboratory, but through the corridor of an ancient castle with stone walls. She enters the underground dungeon of the castle and enters one of the open cells, where she finds a note.
She continues down the corridors, and the walls are covered with a dark red goop that drips disgustingly from them.
From behind a closed cell door, she hears a girl's voice.
Rose goes on in search of the key. She sees an altar on which her name is written in blood, and a candelabra falls on the altar.
Nearby she finds a note.
In a closet nearby she manages to find the key, but she stepped in some red goop.
She notices the goop.
The goop tries to take human form, but it fails. All that comes out is a faint voice…
The goop's leaking out.
Some goop drips down from the ceiling.
She goes to the cell and opens the door. There is no one inside except a puddle of red goop. There is a child's drawing on the floor and Rose picks it up.
The drawing shows three girls being attacked by something large and black. While she is looking at the drawing, a hand appears out of the red goop and grabs Rose's leg.
The goop swallows Rose but someone reaches out and pulls her out.
The girl who rescued her looks like Rose's twin sister, but she has ashy hair. She has black clothes and acts like a real goth, so I'll call her Black Rose.
Rose follows her unexpected companion.
Black Rose approaches the corpse wrapped in a tarpaulin.
Rose helps her push back the corpse, which hides a crack in the stone wall. Black Rose crawls into the crack.
She follows her. They make their way to the back of the dungeon, lit by many candles. In one of the dungeons, Rose sees another self trying to get out of the goop.
She approaches Black Rose trying to use the switch.
Rose continues to follow her double and discovers a small note on the floor.
The cries and moans of her doppelgangers can be heard from everywhere in the different cells. Black Rose disappears somewhere, and Rose has to look for her. Sometimes she discovers her own corpses.
When she enters one of the small rooms, she is attacked by a barely living double with a twisted face. In disgust and horror, Rose tosses her aside and the doppelganger dies.
She takes the handle out of the broken switch lying in the back room, then tries to find her way back. She hears a scream.
When she arrives at the scream, she sees a pale monster only remotely resembling a human grabbing Black Rose. He sort of sucks part of her face out.
She dies.
The monster runs to Rose.

She falls to the floor and covers her face with her hand, which emits sparks of white light. The sparks throw the monster away, but they don't kill it. Rose can't do the trick again. She runs to the switch and inserts the handle she found.
The door, for some reason, doesn't open right away. Rose has to run from the monster.
Finally, the door opens and Rose runs on, but behind the door there are also many monsters.
She runs into a round room with a bas-relief of antique soldiers with bows.
The monsters have already caught up with her and are slowly approaching the trapped prey.
She presses against a wall that suddenly disappears.
Rose continues to run following the directions of the white sparks that form into words.

Rose manages to escape the monsters and enter the house itself. It turns out to be Dimitrescu's Castle. Rose climbs out of the fireplace and into the living room. She sits on the floor, trying to process the horror she has experienced.
Rose follows the advice and begins to search the house. She finds the corpse of another doppelganger lying on the floor.
She keeps walking and hears a voice coming from afar.

Rose enters the main hall and walks up the stairs, still hearing laughter. She arrives at the massive door and sees another of her doppelgangers being dragged by the leg of a monster. He drags her to a fat man in a mask, who is easily recognizable as the Duke.
He touches her face with his fat hand, which against it looks like the hand of a giant.
Several monsters devour it, turning it into a goop.
Hearing this, she inadvertently opens the door behind which she was standing.
He snaps his fingers.
She has to escape again. She finds her way out of the castle.
However, the iron gate slams shut right in front of her.
She runs up the stairs on which the words appear.
She finds a relatively safe place.
She touches the words and a gun materializes in her hands.
Rose kills her pursuer and hears the Duke's voice coming from above.
She approaches a statue of four women, one of which is holding a lamp. It shows a scene where three women kill the fourth lying in a field of roses.
Rose reads the description.
She walks into the dining room and sees a shotgun hidden in a locked cabinet.
The door further is chained.
She finds Bolt Cutters.
Cutting the chain, Rose finds herself in a snow-covered courtyard, in the center of which she sees a masked statue that is encircled by a red flower.
Rose continues her journey and once again enters the house where she finds a rather creepy door with three eyes.
She goes up to the second floor, where some of the passages are filled with red goop. When she tries to step on them, they begin to suck Rose in.
She has to fight several monsters again. She also finds another flower.
Soon she stumbles upon a large amount of goop blocking the passage.

She climbs up the improvised rafters and throws down a ladder to help her get to the other side. After wandering around the dilapidated castle, Rose reaches the attic where she finds the RW-Variant Flask 1.
She finds another note.
Rose approaches the nearest flower.
The white light emanating from her hands dries the flower. It crumbles into dust.
Now the red goop is no longer a serious obstacle for her. She has been given the opportunity to fight. Destroying the flowers in her path she returns to the statue.
Not far from her on the floor, she finds a picture in the next statue wearing a mask.
She destroys the flower holding the statue and gets a bronze mask.
Rose returns to the house.
She destroys the flower blocking the door and continues to explore the castle. She finds a diary, apparently belonging to the Duke.
After destroying another flower, Rose finds a small back room.
She continues her journey and enters a small room with pictures of different animals arranged around it.
She tries to take away the puzzle of the paintings but fails because there are not enough paintings. Soon she is surrounded again. She hears the Duke's voice coming from the speakers.
The Duke is setting his monsters on her.
Rose runs into the room to which Michael pointed.
Rose climbs into the fireplace and discovers a secret passage there.
Rose picks up RW-Variant Flask 2.
The white energy stops the monsters from moving. They practically freeze while Rose directs her power at them.
With a flash of white energy she repels the monster which allows her to have more time to shoot.
Rose picks up a Sprig of White Sage. This plant restores her fungal powers.
Rose continues her journey and soon finds the Monocular Key and the diary next to it.
Rose runs to the basement door.
She opens the cellar door with one eye on it and hears the Duke's voice.
She hears something growling.
Going further, she finds a note.

She solves a riddle with moving pedestals on which the mutilated corpses of her doppelgangers are placed. The riddle opens a door in the floor where the pool of blood used to be. Rose reads the inscription under the painting.
Rose goes down into the dark cellar. She finds another piece of paper lying on the floor next to the mutilated corpse of her double.

Rose walks on and finds herself in a wine cellar, where a huge multifaced monster with a sledgehammer slowly walks by. Rose hides in the shadows, trying not to attract his attention. In the depths of the cellar she finds a statue with a silver mask on it. She also finds a pipe bomb.
Rose begins to be chased by red goop and monsters again. As she escapes, she blows a bomb through them and escapes, exiting the door of the blood pool. She enters a small room.
Michael hands her some ammunition and weed. After searching the room, Rose discovers a lost snake painting and a note apparently left by one of her doppelgangers.
Rose goes out to the courtyard.
He unleashes the ugly monster with a spiky sledgehammer from the basement.
She escapes from the monster and enters the room with the paintings. After placing them in the right order, she opens the lock and gains access to the chest behind it.
In the chest she finds the Triocular Key, a key with three eyes.
With this key she gains access to the shotgun from the dining room.
She opens a door with three eyes and, after defeating several monsters, finds a golden mask.
Her right arm is starting to hurt…
While she was distracted, the monster with a sledgehammer sneaks up on her and takes her away. She ends up in a prison cell.
The Duke speaks to her through the television, which shows his emblem-mask with four eyes and a crown.
Two dozen flowers appear next to Rose.
The rose begins to destroy the flowers one by one.
The Duke releases his monsters.
She escapes from prison.
He hands her some ammunition as a gift.

She approaches the statues and places all three masks on their faces. Above its heads, three lamps light up in blue. Rose takes the Crystal and hears laughter. A laughing fat man appears right at her back.
The crystal crumbles in Rose's hands.
He snaps his fingers.
Rose is sucked in by the red goop.
The Duke laughs watching her torment. Rose comes to her senses in the catacombs. She looks at the installation of four corpses tied to wooden sticks connected together. The Duke sits above her.
The monster with a spiky sledgehammer enters the room.
She attacks the monster and its weak spot opens up, glowing with a white light. Rose shoots at it, dealing damage.
The monster falls to the stone floor.
He unleashes some of the usual monsters into the arena. And on top of that the monster with a sledgehammer gets on his feet…
Michael comes to Rose's rescue.
She picks up RW-Variant Flask 3.
Rose uses her powers to attack the monster's weak spots.
Rose wins again. This makes the Duke very angry.
He snaps his fingers and puddles of red goop appear around Rose.
He points her to the edge of the cliff.
Rose jumps into the water and plunges back into the depths seeing windows around her with figures in them. She comes to her senses outside on a snow covered road.
She enters the gate and walks down a long, creepy alley of dried out plants, bare trees with rusty metal fences. Soon she bumps into a toy monkey with a note in his hands.
The next note runs in an empty baby stroller rolling down the road.
The third note is written on the tombstone.
She enters the building and takes the elevator.
The light in the elevator shuts off abruptly.
The elevator changes in seconds!
She gets out of the elevator and sees two huge stuffed monkeys at the entrance and the sign above the door made with inflatable letters "Welcome Rose".
She begins to investigate the room in which she finds a strange diary.
She continues down the corridors with peeling whitewash.
She walks into a room filled with plush monkeys! On the table is a cake with three candles and a cage with the Crystal glittering in it.
Suddenly the light in the room goes out.

When the lights come back on, the room changes. The toy monkey lies in front of the rose like a patient on the operating table. The whole room basically resembles a hospital. Rose discovers that her belongings are missing.
She's looking at the toy monkey with her tummy stitched up.
She begins to explore the house in search of something sharp. Soon she finds a child's drawing with an inscription on it.
Rose opens the combination lock with a drawing and takes the scissors from the closet. When she returns to the plush monkey in the hallway there are doll arms hanging from the ceiling.
Rose opens the plush monkey and pulls out the Relief of a Child statuette.
A red goop starts flowing out of the monkey and the lights go off again.
The room changes again.
She reads the note lying next to the diorama that appeared. It depicts the burning of a witch in the woods by three girls.
Rose picks up a doll named Lucy.
Rose sets out again on her quest.
She finds another table with a diorama, this time a bathroom scene.
Need another doll!
She finds a door with a bas-relief of the mother and inserts a baby figure into it. In the room he finds a doll…and another diorama table, a gift scene. The voice laughs at her.
Rose uses the found kitchens on Table 2 - the bathroom scene - and gets Jimmy's Room Key. The diorama represents a small episode in Rose's life when she was humiliated in the bathroom.
She goes to Jimmy's room, which turns out to be a creepy basement with a well, and finds a third doll there.
A mannequin that appears out of nowhere scares Rose.
She goes to the third table-the gift scene-and arranges the dolls. It's a little scene from Rose's life again. The box that opens rewards Rose with a new doll.
She goes to the first table-the scene of the burning of the witch in the woods-and arranges the dolls.
Rose gets the Breaker Box Key.
The lights go off again and the scene changes. A telephone appears in front of Rose and begins to ring.
However, the voice on the other end of the phone hangs up and Rose has no choice but to go to the elevator.
Mannequins with scary glowing eyes start chasing Rose and only move when you're not looking at them.
Creepy mannequins begin to follow Rose everywhere. Each time, there are more and more of them. Finally, Rose enters the elevator. She presses the button several times, but the doors don't close.
She hears the child's laughter again. The elevator begins to move downward.
Rose walks out into the room…with huge dolls. Now she herself is the size of a doll.
Several dolls with knives and sickles were passing by Rose.
Rose cautiously sneaks past the armed dolls without letting themselves be discovered.
Rose continues to hide.
Rose runs into the bedroom and finds herself with several giant dolls who sort of play with her.
But the dolls only laugh at her. She runs out of the room chased by a mannequin and closes the huge door.
She's running away from the huge doll heads.
She leaves the creepy world of dolls, returning to the normal world with monsters.
Michael teleports her to Ethan and Mia's house from the beginning of the original game.
She finds a little note by the couch.
She examines the family photo next to the kitchen.
Rose inspects bottles of Regina Rosie wine from Crama Barbaneagra (the name of the winery). The wine is obviously Romanian.
She examines a CD of songs by Miss D & The Pallboys.
She examines her bottle of baby formula.
Rose goes into the closet and examines the jars of baby food.
Rose goes up to the second floor. She starts up the music box.
She goes into her parents' room on the second floor.
She examines her crib.
She goes into the next room where Ethan's office was located. She turns on his computer.
She examines her childhood photo.
She picks up the Drawer Key from behind the photo.
She reads the letter.
She wipes away her tears as she reads the letter. At that moment, a little girl dressed in black comes in. She is the one who has been leading Rose the whole time. Her face seems familiar…
Rose begins to get sucked in by the red goop.
Rose goes back to the house with the dolls.
She goes after Everline trying to avoid the red goop.
Dolls with sickles appear in front of Rose's face and begin to inflict small cuts on her until she waves them away.
She fights crowds of mannequins using her powers.
Rose runs up to the elevator and Eveline appears in front of her.
She grabs Rose's hand.
Suddenly Eveline is enveloped in a white glow. It's Michael.
He teleports Eveline away. Rose runs into the elevator.
He hands Rose several bottles of healing fluid and RW-Variant Flask 4.
Rose hides behind an overturned table not far from Eveline.
Rose attacks Eveline with her fungal powers!
Rose continues to attack Eveline by hiding behind overturned furniture. Eveline takes off and more and more transforms the environment into a real fungal hell.
She grabs her head and explodes with a white glow. After that, the house returns to normal. Eveline sits on the floor and cries. Rose slowly walks up to her.
A gust of wind knocks Rose off her feet.
Suddenly, for a split second, in a burst of white sparks, Ethan appears on the screen and pushes Rose into the abyss through the door.
Rose sinks back into the water, floating past the windows. She finds herself outside again.
She walks past furniture lying in the snow and a door with three eyes.
It overlooks a communal area with a castle in the distance. There are bubbles of goop hanging in the sky, from which it drips down in a trickle.
She notices…herself in the distance. Crows fly into the air and the figure leaves.
She follows the figure through the burning village.
It attacks its monstrous clones with fungal powers.
After breaking through her monstrous copies, she jumps into a cave where she finds a creepy creature remotely resembling a heart. The creature spits out copies of Rose.
She jumps to her heart's content and enters Miranda's lab and reads the diary lying on the table.
Rose checks her weapons and goes deep into the cave.
Along the way, Rose finds a piece of paper.
She approaches the altar and reads the book placed on it.
She goes to Crystal, lying on a post nearby.
She touches the Crystal and the white veins in her arms disappear.
In front of the post, Miranda appears laughing out of the red goop, spreading her black wings.
She slowly hides the Crystal behind her back.
A flashback shows us that Rose was sitting alone on that bench at the beginning of the game.
Tentacles made of goop emerge from the ground. Miranda transforms into her battle form. Suddenly Ethan appears and covers Rose with himself.
She rushes after Rose and the materialized Ethan. Ethan runs ahead and uses the shotgun to blast away the monsters.
The passage begins to crumble. Ethan helps Rose avoid the collapse. They pull away from Miranda for a while.
They keep running.
They run to the exit of the cave. Suddenly Miranda catches up with them and grabs Rose.
Ethan shoots Miranda with a shotgun right in the head.
They run out of the cave, but Miranda continues the pursuit and grabs Rose again.
Ethan covers his daughter with himself again.
He fights Miranda.

Rose was about to run away, but Miranda stabs Ethan with her tentacle and Rose stops. She gathers her will, turns around, and with a scream smashes the Crystal into the frozen ground, regaining her strength.
The epic battle begins!
Rose dodges Miranda's attacks with her new teleport ability!
Rose inflicts tangible damage on Miranda that she falls to the ground.
Rose seizes the moment and runs up to Miranda and shoots her in the face with her shotgun.
The fight exhausts Rose. She hears her father's voice.
He gives her his powers, but it drains him greatly.
Rose charges her new power and knocks Miranda out of the sky with her own tentacles.
She dries Miranda the same way she did with the flowers. At the end she shouts out…
She turns to her father, lying on the ground and dying for the second time.
She cries and they hug.
The screen floods with white light and Rose wakes up in the lab chair.
She hesitantly extends her hand forward as if trying out reality for a test, or trying to use her fungal powers.
Her father's wedding ring remains in her hands.

The next scene. Rose riding the bus and reading Village of Shadows, a children's book that Mia used to read to her. The scene repeats the final scene of the original game, which tells us that the events of the DLC took place before the credits of the original.
The bus stops. We see it’s number - 8 ROWSPINE/SANTA—
Rose walks out of the bus. She arrived at the cemetery. She is holding a bouquet of beautiful white flowers.
The tombstone reads - ETHAN WINTERS a kind husband and loving father who put family before all else.
Someone in a black SUV honks at her.
She walks towards the SUV.
The girl grabs the man in black by the tie.
The SUV drives away.
CREDITS

We arrive at the threshold of the Federal Bureau of Investigation’s Midwest Field Point building. Outside, the weather is bright, it’s sunny summer, and birds are singing. A black man walks with confidence through the corridors towards the workspace of our main heroine, who is toiling over a report on the fourth victim of an unknown disease. Our main heroine’s desk is piled high with various folders. It seems she has been immersed in her work for a long time. Tearing herself away from the report for a moment, she reads a newspaper clipping stating that the quarantine in the destroyed Raccoon City zone has been extended until 2040.
He knocks on the file cabinet to get her attention.
She clumsily takes a folder of documents, dropping the rest.
She rushes into an office where her boss is already waiting. The expression on his face bodes nothing good.
He hands her a folder.
Grace sighs heavily—this place seems to mean something to her.
Grace stands and stares at the floor.
Grace gives a weary shake of her head.

Next scene. Grace walks through rainy city streets, searching for the place. She checks the maps on her phone. She is so lost in thoughts that she doesn’t notice the many passersby on the streets and collides with one of them, a rough-looking man in glasses and a hat.
The first chapter of the story begins.
Grace goes to the hotel.
Wrenwood City Guide

Grace approaches the hotel. It feels as if the place has been abandoned for a long time and is shrouded in blue plastic sheeting. The entrance is also blocked by classic yellow police tape. Grace hesitates at the entrance, not daring to go in.
A police officer in a raincoat approaches her.
Then Grace remembers she’s from the FBI and fumbles in her pockets for the credentials.
Cole lifts the tape, allowing Grace to pass inside.
Grace is completely absorbed in herself and doesn’t react to Cole at all.
He lifts the blue plastic sheeting.
Grace enters a narrow alley and makes her way through piles of construction debris.

She enters the hotel building through the back door used by the kitchen. She turns on her flashlight. It feels as if no one has been here for years. She stops at the door leading from the kitchen. The door’s window is broken.
She gathers her courage and opens the door. After looking around a little, she opens the report given to her by her boss.
Investigation Report

Grace examines the bar, located just past the kitchen. Police markers are everywhere. The building is in a very poor state and is deteriorating before her very eyes. Everywhere there is mold, dust, dirt, and the smell of rotting wood. The mere thought of touching most surfaces is disgusting. On the bar counter, Grace finds a photograph of herself with a key taped to the back and an inscription.
She peels off the key, and at that moment, someone locks the back entrance to the building. Grace, already frightened, becomes terrified. Her voice cracks with fear.
She frantically rummages in her briefcase and pulls out a gun, preparing to meet the stranger. Long seconds pass, but no one comes.
She uses the key to open a door leading into the hotel corridors and heads towards room 204.
Hotel Pamphlet
Grace hears someone’s footsteps coming from the upper floor... But she continues on anyway.
Notice of Closure
As she passes a fireplace, a deer skull falls off its mantelpiece and startles Grace.
Finally, she finds room 204. Someone has turned on a radio inside, but it works with heavy interference.
On the bed lie many photos of Grace and her mother.
One of the photos captures Grace’s attention—it shows her sitting on the floor with a laptop. This photograph awakens memories in Grace. We are transported back to that day.
Grace sits on the bed typing on a laptop. Her mother enters the room.
Her mother sits on the bed next to her.
She tries to take her daughter’s hand, but Grace pulls her hand away.
Her mother looks out the window at the heavy rain falling outside.
The phone rings.
The man abruptly hangs up.
Suddenly, the lights go out.
She hastily begins to pack her things.
She hands her daughter a compact flashlight.
Her mother urges Grace to be quiet.
She pulls her daughter towards the door.
She takes a gun out of her purse.
Grace realizes the situation has become serious and becomes frightened.
They descend to the first floor. They are met by a greying man wearing glasses and carrying a flashlight.

Suddenly, someone in a black robe with a hood covering their face throws a plastic bag over the manager’s head and strangles him. The killer has a pale face, and their mouth is illuminated by a smile with bloody gums.
The killer stabs the manager in the throat with a knife. Blood spurts brightly, and the manager falls to the floor.
Alyssa shoots the killer several times, and he falls into the next room.
She closes the door to the next room.
Now Grace is not just scared—she is horrified and on the verge of a nervous breakdown.
She hides something behind a painting.
They enter a room, and Alyssa locks the door.
She looks at her daughter with regret... Grace, meanwhile, is in terror, pacing back and forth across the room in a fit of hyperventilation.
She tries to catch her breath.
Alyssa hugs Grace. They are both in tears.

At that moment, behind Alyssa, the killer appears... and plunges a knife into her throat. The gunshot wounds slow him, but he can still move. Blood gushes from the wound in Alyssa’s neck, and she dies almost instantly. Some of the blood splatters onto Grace’s face, much to her horror.

However, the wounds overcome him, and he falls, knocking over a table with a kerosene lamp and a bottle of whiskey. A fire starts. Unable to contain her emotions, Grace collapses in tears and cries of pain beside her mother’s body.
She embraces her mother’s body. We return to the present.

Grace sits tearfully on the floor of that very room 204, where she was with her mother 8 years ago. The photos on the couch are part of the things they left here, and it seems someone has been rummaging through them.
Suddenly, a phone rings. Grace is puzzled but goes to answer it. But it turns out it wasn’t the landline ringing, but a mobile phone lying under the photographs.
No one answers, and she throws the phone onto the bed.
However, Grace remembers her mother’s words about things left here.
Thunderclaps sound outside. Grace walks over to the painting. From behind it, she retrieves a leather case containing a diary.
Alyssa’s Journal
Also in the case, Grace finds a disc with no markings. Suddenly, Cole approaches her. He is holding his neck and is silent.
His voice sounds quiet and somewhat strained, as if he is forcing the words out.
The hand that was holding his neck goes limp, hanging like a whip. Cole’s body begins to crackle as if the bones inside are breaking.
Cole’s gums are bleeding heavily, and he spits several teeth into his palm.
He attacks Grace and grabs her arm. She fights back fiercely, but her arm gets caught on a luggage cart.
She tries to aim her gun at him, but Cole knocks it out of her hand.

Cole pulls out a police shotgun, and a brutal struggle begins within himself. He tries to stop the zombie inside him from shooting the trapped Grace. Eventually, she manages to free herself, grabbing the fallen back-door key along the way. She picks up some metal rod, trying to fend off Cole.
The zombie inside Cole overcomes him, and he loses more and more humanity, even biting the metal rod.
Zombie-Cole even manages to take a big bite out of Grace’s arm, after which she breaks free and runs to the second floor.

Quickly opening the stairwell door, she begins to fight Zombie-Cole by the window, smashing it. Picking up a shard of glass, she stabs Cole in the neck with it and pushes him out into the street. Catching her breath and examining the wound on her arm, she notices... a lit light bulb on a nightstand. Someone else is here!

Swallowing her saliva and overcoming her fear, she climbs higher up the stairs and approaches a door, which she carefully opens. Behind the door, she finds a private room where a man with long dark hair sits in a soft leather armchair. He is listening to a gramophone, and beside him lies a strange mechanical mask with magnifying lenses.
The man’s body is as pale as the zombies’ and covered with scars.
He puts on his mask.
The man rises from his chair. He is a veritable giant, at least compared to our heroine, who tries to flee, but the door is closed. The man puts her in a chokehold, and Grace loses consciousness.

A stern man in army boots walks through the dark night streets. He is an investigator. He is at a crime scene—a body lies on the asphalt covered with plastic sheeting. He speaks to a female coordinator via a concealed radio.
He gets into a car.
The investigator picks up his phone and sees a photo sent to him—it is the same giant who is holding Grace.
He looks at his aged and tired face in the rearview mirror.
He starts the car and drives towards the hotel. As he pulls up, Victor is already carrying Grace out of the building over his shoulder.
He notices Victor with Grace on his shoulder emerging from the alley.
Victor stops and watches Leon trying to reach him through heavy traffic. A concerned passerby, seeing Grace, asks Victor a question.
He pulls out a dart gun and shoots the passerby, as well as several other random bystanders. Leon draws his gun and runs towards them. Victor smiles and walks away.
He notices that same concerned passerby, who now needs help herself. He asks a random guy on the street for assistance.
She tackles the guy onto the pavement.
She kisses him, then transforms and bites off his nose. Not yet fully transformed, she is still somewhat aware of herself.
Leon shoots her in the head and fells her with an epic spinning kick. Afterwards, he approaches and chops off her head with an axe.
More zombies appear, which Leon also dispatches without issue. Panic erupts on the street.
Leon shoots an attacking zombie in the head.
Leon removes his left glove, revealing black veins like a spider’s web underneath. He clenches his hand into a fist, as if gathering his strength.
He shakes his head, agreeing with the coordinator. We switch to Grace, who is somewhere in the darkness.
She lies on a bed, her hair pulled up, her face sweaty. A vessel is connected to her vein, draining her blood.
Her hands and feet are tied to the bed as if in a psychiatric hospital.
We realize she is suspended upside down.
She notices the blood container. It is made of glass.

She pulls on the tube, toppling the stand holding the container, shattering it. Using a shard, she cuts through her bindings and rips them from her wrists. Due to blood loss, she has little strength, but she uses it all fighting for her life. Finally, she tips her overturned bed and frees her legs, nervously watching the door.
She somehow manages to stand up and look around.
Progress Notes
She leaves the room.

Grace begins a perilous journey in the darkness. Initially, she manages to find an empty glass bottle, which can be used to distract enemies. She finds an exit fairly quickly, but it is blocked by a metal grate, and the button that opens it doesn’t work. From a distance, the voice of a nurse talking on the phone can be heard.
Grace finds a lighter and a key, then enters a children’s room, where she finds a rather creepy children’s book.
Harriett Jones - SHADOW GHOST
Note on Cardboard Box
Upon opening another door, a mangled body falls onto Grace. Grace checks for a pulse.

At that moment, a huge monstrous hand reaches for the corpse and takes it away. The hand belongs to a giant zombie which, after taking a big bite from the corpse, sniffs at Grace, who is afraid even to breathe. Seizing the moment, Grace flees from the three-meter-tall monster, almost on all fours.
Quite quickly, Grace finds herself in a position where she has nowhere left to run.

Suddenly, a certain system for controlling the monster, built into the walls next to a door, activates, shooting steam and burning the creature’s face. It roars and crawls away to the floor above through a hole in the ceiling.
She returns to the room where she encountered the monster to search it for useful items. There she finds a locker stuffed with the remains of corpses.
She finds a syringe with a healing medicine.
She tries to act as quietly and carefully as possible, not attracting the monster’s attention, but when she moves a cart, a metal container falls off it.
The monster is already close, somewhere outside the window.
She finds green herb.
She finds a screwdriver.

Now she must quietly leave the room in pitch darkness without attracting the monster’s attention. However, the monster detects her anyway, and Grace has to move her feet as fast as possible. She finds a place where a fuse is housed, unscrews the screw with the screwdriver, and removes it. But the monster is lurking. While Grace was unscrewing the bolt, it was creeping down from the ceiling.
She runs at full speed towards the closed iron grate from where she heard the nurse’s voice.

She quickly inserts the fuse and, without waiting for the grate to fully rise, crawls under it. But the monster is already near, and despite the steam in its face, it grabs Grace’s leg and drags her away. Grace fights with all her might, but she cannot break free, and the creature drags the girl into the darkness...

Leon pulls up to the sanatorium. It is a massive, richly decorated building with columns, carved ornaments on the walls, and massive mahogany doors. He gets out of the car and approaches the main entrance, noticing an external surveillance camera. Entering, Leon approaches the reception desk, which is flanked on both sides by a staircase. A nurse appears right behind him.
He follows the nurse through the sanatorium corridors. Rain pours outside, and thunderclaps are heard.
The nurse leads Leon to Victor’s office.

She leaves, closing and locking the door. Leon enters the office, examining it, while Victor himself watches him through a camera in the eye of a skeleton. Leon sees a photo of Victor (who looked relatively normal at the time of the picture) and other doctors. After a few minutes, a message comes over the radio.
A nurse runs into the office.
A doctor enters the room, clearly infected, but the nurse doesn’t notice.

He starts a chainsaw hidden under his coat and rams the blade into the nurse. She dies horribly in a fountain of blood. Zombie-Doctor turns his attention to Leon, but Leon, being a trained fighter, quickly increases the distance by kicking a chair and then dodging the chainsaw.
He blocks the chainsaw with his axe and kills the zombie with a shot from his powerful pistol. The chainsaw continues spinning on the floor. He picks it up.
He mows down approaching zombies with the chainsaw, creating a veritable bloodbath. He impales some, splattering everything around with blood.
He saws through the thick bolt of a door lock with the chainsaw, breaking it in the process. Afterwards, he contacts the coordinator.
And then Leon approaches the metal grate... just at the moment Grace crawls out from there!
We see the scene of Grace being dragged into the darkness again, but now it has a continuation—Leon shoots the horrific creature right in the head.

Grace runs towards Leon as he shoots the creature, now standing up, in the head again... and several more times, turning its head into a bowl of bloody mush with a dangling eye. Grace, holding her head in her hands, tries to calm down.

Someone presses a button, and the grate slams shut, separating the heroes—Grace is now closer to the exit, and Leon remains inside. Not only the doors but also the windows are blocked. Complete lockdown.
Leon hands her a gun. And not just any gun, but his own massive cannon.
Grace takes the pistol, holding it with both hands.

The door between them closes, plunging the corridor into complete darkness. Leon pulls out a flashlight and looks around, realizing that just a few meters away from him stood Victor, now wearing a hooded robe resembling snakeskin.
Leon attacks him hand-to-hand, but Victor grabs him by the throat with lightning-fast reflexes.
He examines Leon with his helmet.
Despite his resistance, Leon loses consciousness.
Naturally, Grace’s first attempt is to get out through the main entrance, but she needs to collect three rhombus-shaped keys, symbolizing the words Moon, Sun, and Star.
Soon she finds her first safe room, with a typewriter and an item storage box.
Care Center Pamphlet
Whiteboard Scrawl

Grace enters the next room, above which a red lamp glows. Turning on the light, she notices a corpse in the corner... but it doesn’t scare her as much as before. In the far part of the room, she sees a glass prison holding a little girl.

The girl’s hair is the same silver as Grace’s. She is reading a book and is slightly surprised by the stranger’s arrival. The corpse in the room doesn’t seem to bother her at all. This is because the book is written in Braille.
The girl lifts her nearly completely white eyes from the book.
She is a little frightened.
She gets up from the bed and falls to the floor.
She examines the girl.
Grace scans the room, noticing another glass prison.
She turns to the girl.
Suddenly, the corpse comes to—not as a zombie, just a person who lost consciousness from their wounds.
The doctor is very rude and serious.
The doctor dies. Grace picks up his card from the floor.
Having obtained the key to the West Wing, Grace heads there. Making her way through the corridors, she enters a kitchen, where she finds a monstrous zombie chef.
He is engrossed in chopping meat.
She distracts him with a cart of soup—the chef, for some reason, plunges his hand into it.
Grace manages to slip behind the chef’s back and exit into the adjacent corridor.
This zombie seems preoccupied with turning off the lights in the corridor. Apparently, that was his duty in life, and now it’s a residual brain reaction.
He tries to attack Grace with a flashlight, but she dodges.

Grace emerges into a dining hall. A long table in the center of the room is piled with food and corpses. And sitting on this table, on all fours, is a zombie, devouring it all. The tablecloth is stained with blood, saliva, and juices. Grace finally finds a suitable pistol for herself, a B934, and hastily leaves the horrific room.

She manages to find a few bullets, which she promptly unloads into a zombie that attacks her, then finishes it off with a knife made from half of a pair of scissors. By a fortunate twist of circumstances, someone had already tried to kill this zombie with a knife, so she extracts this useful item from its body. She finds a few more bullets, and now she has the means to defend herself. She approaches a door leading to some kind of basement, which is secured with a valve lock, but when she tries to turn the large red valve, it simply falls off its mounting, becoming useless.
She ascends to the upper floors, where she spots a zombie singer in the distance.
Folded Note
When Grace kills another zombie, something interesting happens—part of the human consciousness remains, and the zombie, though unintelligibly, utters the following:
Returning to the safe room and resting a bit, Grace continues her search for an exit.
Copy of an Email to a Manager
Note to the Chairman
Grace finds a pencil and reveals the last entry in the diary, which turns out to be an important clue for a safe.
Chairman’s Note
Opening the safe, Grace obtains the Moon Quartz! She immediately wants to place it in its designated spot to save inventory space.
She uses the found ruby to access the gaming hall.
She finds the East Wing Keycard after searching a hand... just a severed hand, lying under a bed.
And then the Zombie Chef comes for her! Grace needs to run. Along the way, she even manages to kill the Zombie Singer. And on the bar counter lies another gun—an S&S M232.
She exits into the central corridor and opens the East Wing. Afterwards, she returns again to the blind girl...

She goes to the East Wing and finds the medical/surgical department. There, she manages to find a syringe that kills infected cells. She tests it on a nearby zombie doctor, and its head simply explodes.
She finds that same zombie who stood by the light switch...

...and shoots it in the head. A bit further, in a laboratory, Grace finds a rather interesting device that allows her to collect blood from the infected, enabling its study and processing into various useful items.
She immediately analyzes the blood sample she has.
Blood Compound Synthesis
Lead Researcher’s Message

Grace continues to explore the East Wing and finds a key-bracelet on a researcher’s corpse. Suddenly, something unimaginable happens—a huge, fat zombie, vaguely resembling a giant child, rolls into the room.
It smashes through load-bearing columns in the corridor and lumbers after Grace. Outrunning it proves fairly easy—the creature is clumsy and cannot keep up with slim, young Grace.
Victor’s Journal Entry
Grace decides to eliminate the threat posed by the fatso, Chunk, and injects him with the infected cell destroyer while he moves through the corridors, but nothing happens.
Grace has to run through the corridors collecting blood for a new batch of the destroyer. She encounters a Zombie Janitor who is distressed by blood stains on the floor.
She receives a dose of lead medicine from Grace, after which our heroine descends into the basement. An excavator comes towards her, crashing through a wall.
She shoots the driver, and the excavator stops, blocking half the corridor.
She crawls under the excavator.
Having gathered everything useful, Grace continues to explore the wing.
Disposal Team Report Assignment
Background Check
Bar & Lounge Safe Code
She takes antique coins from the safe, using them to purchase several upgrades. Then she encounters a strange zombie...
Safe Replacements
Examination Room Safe Code
Grace returns to the surgical wing of the sanatorium in search of the second key to the main entrance.
Grace kills a whole brigade of doctors turned into zombies, then finds a bottle of concentrated acid in a back room.
Patient Medical Records
On her way, Grace examines an office where she encounters... Victor. She immediately aims her gun at him.
Grace continues to stammer, which tells us she is frightened.
He steps closer and literally towers over Grace. The gun pointed at him does not bother him in the slightest.
A beep comes from the transmitter.
We see a certain man lying on a cot in an isolation ward, infected, his face covered with a rag. Blood begins to seep from a wound on his arms, and the man breathes slowly and heavily.
Frightened, Grace flees the office and wedges a chair under the door handle. This does not stop Victor, and he lunges after her. Grace hides behind a bedside table.
He leaves, and Grace carefully emerges from her hiding place.
She uses the acid to corrode the lock on a safe and, after entering the correct symbol combination, obtains the Sun Quartz and a leather-bound diary.
Spencer’s Research I

Grace runs back to the door and inserts the second symbol, then opens a box located right in the nearby safe room. Inside the box is an artificial lung, which can be used on a corpse in the surgical wing. The corpse revives, and Grace must return it to its dead state, after which she receives a second-level key-bracelet. With it, she goes to a new part of the medical wing.
Double Mutation
Nurse’s Log
In the dead doctor’s office, Grace finds a diary containing a clue for the third key.
However, the images on the buttons have long since worn off.
Examining the buttons, Grace realizes they have hints marked in Braille.

She remembers the blind girl again. By the way, on the dead doctor’s table, there is also a photograph of this girl, and next to her is her sister, who looks about the same as the girl herself, but her hair is not silver, but dark.
Grace remembers that the girl is secured behind a third-level lock, and on the doctor’s wrist, there is also a third-level access key.
She hesitates, wanting to remove the bracelet from the corpse.
Having removed the bracelet, she puts it in her pocket and looks at the safe.
She returns to the blind girl. Approaching the door, she still hesitates to unlock it immediately, remembering the warning.
Grace unlocks the door and enters.
She takes Emily in her arms and runs with her.
Now Grace must watch over Emily, but while she carries her, her actions are limited.
She manages to reach the doctor’s office in the isolation ward without much trouble.
She puts Emily on the floor and guides her.
Meanwhile, terrible sounds come from the ward...
She closes the office door and heads towards the terrible sounds, reloading her gun.
In the ward, there are several zombies, including the one that lay on the cot covered with a rag. This is the zombie with the large mutated head. During the fight, Grace hears Emily’s voice.
Having finished off the zombies, Grace runs to Emily.
She hands her the last symbol.

But before she can take the symbols from her hands, the wall explodes with a mighty crash, and everything around plunges into blackness. Grace frantically searches for her flashlight... It turns out that the giant monster recently killed by Leon has burst into the room, but it seems the monster’s head has gradually regenerated. The creature grabs the blind Emily and drags her away.
She gives chase. But to follow the monster, she must descend into the pitch-black darkness of the basement. She hesitates. Fear pins her to the ground.
She carefully descends into the black hole.
Summoning her courage, she jumps into the darkness.
We switch to Leon. He is tied to a chair, and a spotlight shines in his face. Victor approaches.
Leon simply turns away.
He picks up a scalpel.
He runs his hand through his hair.
He pulls back the collar of his shirt, exposing the black veins.
Victor examines Leon with his helmet.
He makes an incision on Leon’s neck, right next to the black veins.
While he speaks, Leon carefully cuts his bindings with a secret knife.

Having cut the bindings, Leon kicks Victor against the wall with a powerful blow, then strikes his helmet with his foot. Leon notices a gun lying nearby and lunges for it while Victor is stunned, but Leon himself feels weak. Taking advantage of this, Victor flees. He manages to escape the attic and pulls the ladder up after him, managing to utter one last phrase into the narrowing opening.
Leon immediately calls the coordinator.
Leon injects some kind of substance into his neck and feels relief.
He checks the remaining bullets in his gun.
He turns on his flashlight and heads into the adjacent corridor.
He begins to explore the attic, which is quite spacious.
Satiety Suppression Medical Records
Leon finds an MSBG 500 shotgun... and Chunk devouring a corpse.
Leon brings down the fatso with a few shots and embeds his axe in its head.
After several minutes of running through tight spaces and fighting, Leon inflicts enough damage that Chunk withers and explodes, splattering entrails everywhere.
Basement Safe Code
Leon finds a way to descend to the floor below and get into the main sanatorium building.
Dishwasher’s Note
He encounters the Zombie Chef.
He finds Victor’s office. Inside lies a heap of corpses on the couch by the coffee table and on the floor. Everything is splattered with blood.
He is attacked by a big-headed zombie.
When he activates a secret lever, several zombies burst into the room.
He finds a hidden elevator and descends in it.
We switch to the female coordinator herself, sitting at a computer.
The coordinator reads a document on the screen, and we are shown who has been helping Leon all this time. And yes, we know her.
She scrolls through documents and comes across one that is heavily redacted. Only a few words are visible.
A photo of Grace Ashcroft appears.

We switch to Grace, who has landed rather ungracefully on the ground and is frantically searching for the flashlight that fell out during her fall. Having looked around and found no enemies, she exhales.
She walks through tunnels located at the very bottom of the building. Judging by the dangling chains, someone was held here.
Staff Member’s Last Words
Soon, Grace emerges into concrete corridors and from there into a security room where monitor screens are located. Seeing Emily sitting in a cell on the monitor, Grace sighs.
Operation to Eliminate "The Girl"

Grace continues to explore the dark concrete corridors, only weakly lit by red lamps. She finds cells where zombies are kept, or perhaps where people were kept who simply turned into zombies. Finally, she finds the cell holding Emily, but from inside come some terrible sounds. Carefully approaching and peering in, Grace notices that the tall monster is inside the cell. The monster quickly crawls away through a ventilation shaft in the ceiling. Without wasting a moment, Grace runs to the cell door. Emily seems to be asleep.
Grace gently rattles the cell door to get Emily’s attention without attracting the monster’s. Emily comes to.
However, the girl just turns over on her other side and continues sleeping. Grace examines the cell and sees a huge number of severed doll heads, which, apparently, the monster brought.
Grace examines the cell itself and sees a green light above it, indicating the cell has an electronic lock. Now she needs to find the panel that opens it.
Unlocking the Cells
Grace begins to search every room and every corner for fuses. Besides zombies, she is also opposed by the darkness and the rather complex and confusing navigation through the dirty basement.
Technician’s Note
Security Guard’s Report
Grace finds a room with corpses wrapped in black plastic suspended from a conveyor on the ceiling. She has to move the conveyor to proceed further. Some of the zombies are still capable of movement.
She has to descend into a pool where zombies were drowned. The liquid, half consisting of blood and other bodily fluids, she had previously drained...
The pool turns out not to be a pool at all, but a corpse disposal machine.

In the far part of the pool, massive blades activate, and the pool floor moves like a conveyor belt towards these blades. Some of the zombies are capable of movement, and Grace has to defend herself while pondering what to do next.
She retrieves a fuse located at the other end of the pool.
She has to slide down a pipe filled with rotting water, blood, and bodily fluids, and finally plunge into a vat of such water.
She quickly runs to the panel and activates it using the three fuses. Lights come on, and all the cells open. Grace runs to Emily.
Emily starts to struggle, not understanding who is in front of her.
She hugs Emily.
She takes Emily in her arms and runs towards the opened elevator.
Zombies that have been freed from their cells are everywhere.
She rushes into the elevator.
She tries to move the pallet of bags blocking the door. At that moment, the giant monster appears.
At the last second, she presses the button, and the elevator slowly ascends.
She exhales—the danger has passed. At that moment, something strikes the elevator.

It’s the giant creature—it’s crawling on the outside. The elevator stops, and Grace tries to pry the doors open to get out on the floor where the elevator has halted. She succeeds. First, she lifts Emily up.

She helps her out of the elevator, but as she climbs out herself, the creature grabs her leg and tries to pull her back into the elevator. At this moment, the elevator cable begins to snap under the weight.
Grace realizes that if she ends up in the elevator, she’ll have no chance of survival, and braces herself as best she can. The creature speaks something articulate for the first time.

And then the elevator falls! Grace screams—whether from pain or fear, we don’t know yet. The camera slowly pans down to her feet, and we hold our breath, waiting for her leg to be severed by the elevator... but the elevator only cuts off the creature’s arm. Grace collapses onto the tiled floor with relief. Emily crawls over to her.
She holds out the quartz cube with the star symbol on it.
Emily hugs her. A little taken aback, Grace decides to hug Emily back.
She stands up heavily and takes Emily in her arms.
She walks towards the main entrance (located nearby) to finally leave this cursed place.
She inserts the last quartz cube into the door and steps outside.
She has become somewhat lethargic, as if drained of strength. Grace walks towards the helipad, where a helicopter is standing, next to which is a pilot with an injured leg.
He steps closer.
He slaps his leg.
She puts Emily in the helicopter.
She seats Emily, who immediately lies down to sleep.
She seats Emily and fastens her seatbelt.
Grace takes out her gun and heads into a nearby building. Inside, it looks like an ordinary guest house.
Visitor Record
She enters a meeting room. Besides the wines and expensive furniture, a collection of medical bone saws is also striking.
Note Regarding Research Results
Grace dismantles a wine bottle, finds a secret key, and uses it on a panel in the center. The entire room begins to descend, as if it were an elevator.
She descends into a secret medical research laboratory.
Spencer’s Research II
She enters a spacious room with many capsules filled with red liquid. Some of them contain human beings.
Subject 170 Record
Grace Ashcroft Blood Analysis Report
She finds the helicopter key on a desk in an office by the computers and enters a nearby room, where she accidentally stumbles upon Victor. He doesn’t notice her, and she hides behind a capsule.
He hangs up the phone and takes a briefcase from the table.
He laughs triumphantly and quickly leaves the office. The wail of a siren sounds.
Equipment begins to break down; tubes rip from their sockets, flooding everything with red liquid. Everything sparks and shakes.
Grace runs to the "elevator" she came down on but finds only a huge hole.
She needs to find another exit quickly—she arms herself with something like a fire extinguisher that makes zombies explode when used and charges ahead.
A massive tanker explodes, and Grace is nearly swept away by a wave of red sludge.
She runs with all her might through the flooding corridors.
She finds a staircase, climbs up it to the surface, and emerges outside.
She runs towards the helicopter, which is already being besieged by zombies. Harry is preparing for takeoff.
She runs quickly and boards the helicopter. Harry buckles up and sees zombies breaking through a fence.
Grace moves to the rear of the helicopter and sits down next to Emily.
The helicopter rotor slowly spins up...
Zombies swarm the helicopter. One tries to smash the windshield with its head.

The helicopter lifts off heavily with the weight of zombies clinging to it, tilts to the right, and shakes off several zombies, leaving a bloody trail on the asphalt. However, zombies still remain on the helicopter, and one breaks through to Harry.
Grace grabs the control stick while Harry fights off the zombie.

Despite Grace’s efforts, they crash into the roof of a church and land in a cemetery, a fitting place given how dangerous their landing was. Grace sighs with relief, turns to Harry... and he is impaled by a branch. Grace screams.
It seems the girl is also injured—there’s blood on her shirt, and she’s breathing heavily.
We return to Leon, still standing in Victor’s secret office.
He looks out the window and sees the helicopter crash! He scans the room and notices a bolt-action sniper rifle standing by the table.
He smashes the window with the stock and sees through the sniper scope Grace climbing out of the wreckage. Grace reaches Emily, opening the dented helicopter door with her bare hands.
She unbuckles and carries the girl out, then notices a horde of zombies heading towards them. They are already at the cemetery gates!
Leon also sees the whole situation.
He jumps out of the window onto a roof and calls Sherry, his coordinator.
He steps onto a balcony, a better vantage point for firing, and provides cover for Grace as she carries Emily. Grace tries to shoulder-open the church door.
He kills zombies one by one with precise shots.
Having taken out the last one, he slides across the roof and heads towards Grace, who is now inside the church. He lands on a zombie, crushing its head with his boot.
Leon reaches the church, now engulfed in flames. Emily is in very bad shape, lying on the floor, with Grace, fighting back tears, leaning over her.
She tries to press her hands against the wound on the girl’s chest.
He takes Emily in his arms. Zombies slowly surround the church.
With two precise shots, he kills an approaching zombie.
A slow but very dangerous zombie, pulsating with pus-filled growths, enters the church. It roars ferociously from a huge, toothy maw.
A bloody battle begins in the burning church. Leon picks up a fire axe and uses it to cleave through zombie heads.
He manages to kill the pus-filled zombie, though the fight is not easy. He holsters his gun with relief and goes to get Grace. They need to descend into a dark, gloomy basement. Leon sighs.
Grace runs across the earthen floor of the dirty basement. Emily moans softly in pain.
They end up in the sanatorium’s sewers but later climb higher.
They find themselves in a gigantic warehouse. Grace spots The Girl crawling nearby.
She lays her down on a stack of iron grates.
Roof Hatch Manual
As she leaves, she closes a metal roll-up door behind her to keep others out. Now she needs to deal with The Girl. She opens a hatch on the warehouse roof, but the process stops.
She goes down lower, but The Girl is already there. After the first switch, it grabs Grace by the head and throws her to the floor.

Now The Girl is very close. Grace chooses not to confront it directly, as she lacks the firepower to fight such a creature. She distracts it by throwing empty bottles and using her flashlight, allowing her to reach the switches.
The last switch refuses to turn!
She has to lure The Girl over long distances to have time to wrestle with the stuck handle. And she succeeds!
Now, with light everywhere, The Girl’s eyes hurt, and it runs away.
However, The Girl finds a way to reach her. It slowly walks towards its prey.
She presses a button, and the hatch on the ceiling slowly opens, flooding the entire space with light.
Due to the abundance of sunlight, The Girl begins to smoke. Grace shoots it in the head several times, and it screams madly in pain, melting from within. It turns into a heap of rotten, oozing flesh.
She runs to Emily...
...but the girl seems dead.
No pulse can be felt at her wrist.
She tries to perform CPR, counting the rhythm out loud.
She continues CPR, also breathing air into her mouth. Gradually, she loses control of her emotions.
Grace’s hands slow to a stop. She begins to cry and sinks to the floor.
And then Emily’s hand twitches.

However, the girl begins to scream piercingly, as if a demon has possessed her. She falls to the floor and writhes in convulsions. Afterwards... she mutates. Grace watches the process in horror, on the verge of a nervous breakdown. She slowly crawls backward, towards the door. Emily transforms into a shapeless leech with a claw. With this claw, she smashes everything around.
The creature notices Grace and lunges at her, but Leon runs up and fires several shots, driving the monster away from Grace.
She hangs on Leon’s arm, the one holding the gun.
We switch to Leon—our task is to finish off the creature. Leon doesn’t hesitate and pulls the trigger, despite Grace’s pleas.
The creature falls and dies.
In tears, she crawls towards the shapeless beast.
Grace throws Leon’s huge pistol, which she has been carrying all this time, at his feet.

Grace slowly walks away. Leon wants to bend down for his gun but falls to one knee and vomits blood. With a habitual motion, he wipes the blood from his mouth. Grace walks on, staring at the floor, not even noticing Victor standing nearby.
He presses a button that raises a massive metal door right in front of Grace. It leads outside.

Beyond the door, a middle-aged man in dark sunglasses with slicked-back silver hair awaits her. He wears a white lab coat and a leather cloak draped over his shoulders. Black veins are visible on the man’s face. He smokes a cigarette, wearing leather gloves.
The man gazes into the distance.

A helicopter flies towards them, and we switch to Sherry. She swallows some oxycodone hydrochloride (a strong painkiller) and looks at herself in the mirror, then removes a glove from her hand, on which black veins are also visible. Inhaling, she returns to work.
Meanwhile, Leon is driving in his car.
Leon heads straight for Raccoon City! The city greets him with dust and abandoned buildings.

We get a stunning wide shot of the city, at the center of which yawns a huge crater, partially filled with water, from the nuclear explosion that occurred back in Resident Evil 3. Hello to everyone who saw it in 1999! Personally, I couldn’t contain my emotions. The surrounding area is partially or completely destroyed.
Entering the city, Leon stops and gets out of the car.
Raccoon City
Umbrella
Ozwell E. Spencer
Raccoon City Incident Newspaper
Leon walks through the dusty ghost town, gun at the ready.

Leon continues searching for the way to the epicenter of the blast. He accidentally steps on a human skull, and it awakens memories... at this time, a spider-like monster watches him from a rooftop. It descends silently like Shelob from The Lord of the Rings—incidentally, the monster’s size is comparable to hers.
The spider tries to attack Leon with a leg, but he dodges and strikes the leg with his axe.

He runs from the spider and hides in a building. The spider cannot follow him and, in its attempts to squeeze through, collapses the ceiling, blocking the passage. Now Leon is trapped in the building and needs to find a way out. Meanwhile, the spider searches for a way to get inside... and it succeeds very quickly. It chases Leon down a long corridor and smashes through a wall with a kick of its leg. Leon climbs to the floor above, where he must fight the spider.
Leon pulls out his shotgun and shoots the spider right in the face, then notices growths on its rear and concentrates fire on them. The spider, not expecting such resistance, flees.

The battle continues, and finally the spider tires of it all and pins Leon against the wall, trying to devour him. Leon grabs his axe and starts striking the spider’s chitinous head-plate. They crash through several floors under the creature’s immense weight, and there Leon delivers the final blow with his axe straight into its toothy maw.
He finds himself in a subway tunnel and quickly emerges onto the surface, arriving at massive iron gates leading to the city center.
He looks at the bomb on the gate.
He examines the bomb and realizes the detonator is missing. Leon goes to scout the area.
Orders for the Engineer Corps
Leon boots up a military laptop sitting on a table.

He doesn’t know that someone is watching him through a specially configured camera on the laptop—and it’s Victor, currently flying in a helicopter with a personal pilot, Grace, and the man in glasses.
Meanwhile, Leon continues to explore the surroundings. He enters a massive warehouse.
In answer to his question, several dry zombies attack him. He defeats them and approaches a crane to clear a path.
He emerges onto a roof.
He surveys the warehouse roof.
Leon scans the surrounding rooftops with binoculars.
Leon starts searching the marked locations, fighting, and looking for paths.
Battery Storage Locations
Having collected the batteries, he steps outside, where he discovers several corpses of BSAA soldiers. Leon connects to a camera on a soldier’s helmet to see what happened.
They move out into the street in a combat formation. Someone kills one of the soldiers with what seems like a powerful sniper rifle—the soldier’s head simply explodes.
They begin to die one by one, bursting in fountains of blood.
The last soldier is killed by Zeno. In his hands is a rather large revolver. The recording ends.
We see that Grace and Zeno have landed. Zeno leads Grace somewhere, carrying a briefcase.
Leon takes the container key from the dead squad leader and continues his search.
Inventory List
In one of the containers, Leon finds a motorcycle in excellent condition.
He encounters a zombie with a chainsaw.
After a long wander through the ghost city, Sherry calls Leon again, but this time just to talk.
Leon climbs onto a second roof after a difficult and dangerous journey.
Engineer Corps Note

He ascends to the last and most dangerous roof of a dilapidated building. He has to traverse the wall of an elevator shaft, tilted at 45 degrees, while the elevator cars themselves are still hanging at the top and barely holding on!
The cars crash down, but Leon manages to leap aside. He reaches the very roof.
Finally, he collects the last, third part of the detonator!
He looks around and sees a suspended platform stuck on the opposite building.
He reaches it with difficulty and descends lower, but the platform gets stuck.
A zombie jumps down towards him. Leon shoots it in the head with his shotgun.
The platform breaks loose and hangs precariously over the abyss.
Leon jumps off it and makes his way back into the city.
Leon blows up the gates, gets on his motorcycle, and with a roar of the engine, speeds forward. He rides onto a highway clogged with abandoned cars, carefully weaving between them.

He notices Victor standing on a nearby building. In his hands... a flare gun. He blows up the road in front of Leon, but that doesn’t stop him. So Victor sends three mutant dogs, the size of horses, after him.
He hits the gas, firing from a compact automatic rifle. Victor himself joins the chase on a motorcycle. The mutant wolves block Leon’s path.
He mows them down with the automatic rifle and keeps going. Victor fires a grenade launcher at Leon.
Leon races at breakneck speed on the motorcycle, shooting at new wolves, trying to hit Victor, and, of course, not dying during the next jump or sharp turn. Soon, the road is blocked by metal debris.
The chase continues. Victor uses the grenade launcher again to cut off Leon’s path.
He catches up to Victor, and an adrenaline-fueled duel ensues between them.

He kicks Victor’s motorcycle, and it stops—the axe pierced the gas tank during the duel, and now fuel is leaking from it. Leon stops opposite him. There’s no road ahead, just a cliff. The only way is back, towards Victor.
Leon rides forward and fires. Victor’s motorcycle catches fire, he falls off it and, pinned by the motorcycle, slides into the abyss. The motorcycle explodes.
Leon arrives at the center. His fuel is almost at zero, and he decides to abandon the motorcycle.

After walking a bit, he reaches the gates of his police station as we saw them in Resident Evil 3. He approaches the door and opens it with effort. Ghosts of the past assail him—he sees scenes from Resident Evil 2 Remake.
Even the music subtly reminds you of that very theme. The station is abandoned and covered in a thick layer of dust and small debris.
He looks at that very statue under which the passage was hidden—it is now partially destroyed.

He looks at an aerial photo of Raccoon City dated 05/02/2023 and labeled ARK. Then he examines a banner lying in the dirt reading WELCOME LEON and reads a handwritten note - YOU CAN’T SAVE ANYONE. A nearby laptop with surveillance cameras turns on. On one of the cameras, Leon sees Zeno.
He drops the note on the floor and starts pressing the laptop’s keyboard buttons. A map appears, very similar to the one we saw in the original Resident Evil 2.
Leon draws his gun and goes to save Grace. He walks past Marvin’s desk.
He reads Marvin’s diary.
Top Secret Operation
Record of Events
Leon even encounters a zombie in a police uniform...
He puts his former colleague to rest with a shotgun blast to the head.

He finds a photo of the old S.T.A.R.S. team in the iconic location—the squad’s office. It’s very dark in the office, though, making it hard to make out details. The caption reads "5/24/1996 - with the elite S.T.A.R.S. members"
Barry’s To Do List
Leon finds the very place where he saw Zeno. He hides around a corner and eavesdrops on the conversation, gun at the ready.
He presses a button, and a photo of Emily appears on the screen.
The date on the photo shown is 10-01-2020
The next photo—the same girl, but the date is 12-05-2016
Grace scrolls through photos dated 06-25-2013, 09-17-2010, 11-26-2007, 05-19-2005. The same face is in all of them.
He lights a cigarette.
It dawns on Grace that Emily strangely resembles her.
He shows another video of Emily sleeping in a bed.
We switch to that very "Emily" shown in the video. She is sleeping peacefully but is awakened by the voices of researchers through the door.
Sounds of breaking glass are heard, frightening Emily.
Our new girl’s name is Chloe. She calls out to her friend nearby, but her bed is empty.
She hears screams.
She goes to inspect the room.

She looks at a photo on the wall showing seven girls of roughly similar appearance—due to the photo quality, it’s hard to tell if they are identical or not. Next to them are two male researchers. Chloe leaves the room and discovers, in the next room, a researcher surrounded by five girls... their heads are clean-shaven, and crude surgical scars are visible on them. They smile silently, giggle softly, and their eyes glow with a red light.
He tries to shoot a girl in the head, but the wound heals instantly. The researcher falls to the floor.
The girls reach their hands towards him, and one of them snaps his neck.

The girls turn towards her. Horrified, Chloe closes the door and runs back to her room, hiding under the bed. The girls walk from bed to bed, banging on them with their fists, but they don’t get a chance to search under Chloe’s bed because a nurse’s scream attracts their attention. Chloe continues to explore other rooms. She finds a Black researcher on the floor.

She encounters the girls again and hides. They behave like living dolls—no injuries harm them; they always get up and continue to exist. Chloe finds a hatch key but can’t reach it—those creepy girls are all around.
She quietly slips past them and descends into the basement through the hatch.

She simply walks through dark corridors, quietly crying, eventually reaching some kind of altar behind which the Umbrella symbol is secretly located. At the door, she realizes she can’t reach the ladder that theoretically leads outside; she needs something to stand on to reach it. Looking around, she finds a very suitable object. It’s a small child’s coffin, not just one, but many, stored in a back area near the altar. Inside one, she finds a tag.
Toe Tag

However, a little girl naturally cannot drag this coffin, so she uses it to climb over an iron grate and grab a fire poker. But as she exits the iron gates, other shaven-headed girls start emerging from the coffins. With a scream, Chloe runs towards the ladder. She lowers it with the poker, clambers up, and closes the lid behind her to stop the crawling girls from reaching her.
She seems on the verge of a nervous breakdown. Chloe emerges into the snow-white corridors of the research center.
Series 60 Results
New Researcher’s Notebook

She finds a large photograph on the wall titled "April 15, 1987 - Mr. Spencer’s Visit". In it, there are about 32 girls of roughly similar appearance, although some have different hair colors and hairstyles. Later, Chloe enters a large meeting hall, where she sees photos on a projector that make her understand that people like her are simply grown. While she is engrossed in looking at the photos, a researcher approaches her.
With a swift motion, he injects a sedative into her neck. The girl loses consciousness instantly.
He examines her pupils.
Chloe is picked up and hurriedly carried away. Grace watches all this with a mixture of bitterness and anger.
Zeno replies with some pleasure in his voice.
Grace is shocked.
Grace is already on the verge of a nervous breakdown.
She remembers Alyssa.
Grace is overcome by visions of all the horrors she is the cause of. They claw at her soul like monsters. She screams, and at that moment, Leon bursts in with his gun drawn.
She loses consciousness from the shock. Leon tries to shoot Zeno, but he dodges with lightning speed, much like a certain infamous villain in this game series.
The ceiling collapses, and into the room comes... Mr. X. Zeno picks up Grace.
Zeno flees. Mr. X advances on Leon.
He immediately shoots Mr. X several times in the chest, but it has no effect. He tries to fight him hand-to-hand, but Mr. X is many times stronger and hits like a hammer.
He fires his large-caliber pistol, burying Mr. X under a pile of debris.
Now Leon must leave the police station as quickly as possible, as Mr. X is hunting him!
Leon reaches the main entrance. It seems Mr. X has stopped pursuing.
Leon exits into the city and heads towards the orphanage. He enters the former iconic Gun Shop Kendo and examines a flyer.

He sees that very basement and remembers the moment when Robert Kendo himself defended his daughter, who had become a zombie. Leon slowly opens the door and finds only bones behind it. He remembers...
Leon notices a prescription for pills for Emma, Robert’s daughter, issued by the Umbrella Corporation.
Leon ponders what happened back then for a long time.
A crash is heard from afar.
He goes outside and sees the cause of the crash—Mr. X, standing on a roof, has torn a missile from a downed helicopter with his bare hands.

He runs, hides behind a car, but the blast still throws him a considerable distance. Mr. X jumps down to Leon. The arena is engulfed in flames, and there’s nowhere to run. And Mr. X has another missile... he even seems to be smiling.

He shoots the missile, and it explodes. Mr. X merely mutates slightly and loses his clothes. The fight begins! Mr. X hurls cars, and Leon doesn’t spare his arsenal against such a monster. He even manages to embed his axe in its chest.
A pulsating, pus-filled sore opens on Mr. X’s chest. Leon baits it into striking the ground and dodges. Mr. X’s mutated hand gets stuck in the ground, allowing Leon to shoot the weak point.
A fountain of blood erupts from Mr. X’s chest, its ribs crack inward, and it dies, falling to the ground.
He picks up the remaining ammo and heads towards the orphanage, which is in a very sorry state—part of the building has literally collapsed.

A very difficult path lies ahead, as all direct routes have long been buried. He has to leave the building and re-enter it several times, even traversing a half-destroyed train car, where a mutated plant attacks him.
He kills the plant with one precise shot, but afterwards, the car begins to fall into the abyss, as that very plant was literally holding it up. Leon nimbly jumps from the falling car.
He finds a thick military cable tied to a post.
Using the cable, he descends into dark, root-infested depths.
In the gloomy caves, Leon must fight predatory flowers of various sizes. At the end, a gigantic, horrific version awaits him!
He shoots barrels of flammable mixture under the plant. The plant burns in agony, revealing access to a massive circular door leading to the laboratory. Leon plunges into total darkness.
Moving a bit further, Leon enters the snow-white, sterile corridors of the research center. The design is quite futuristic. He moderately explores the empty corridors, data centers, and laboratories.
N0-AH Status Report
Finally, Leon finds a security post where monitors show an overview of the entire building, as well as surveillance cameras. He locates a place on the map marked as Pandora.
He notices on the cameras that several soldiers are running through the corridors towards him.

He slings his rifle off his shoulder and goes to meet the guests. An adrenaline-fueled, tense battle begins with elite guards in a darkened data center. Only thanks to his honed skills does Leon survive. He enters a vast room designed as an exhibition. In many rooms, people have been turned into art installations.
